exits.label: ◂ back up to the Night Market

npcs.name: Sela
npcs.title: the soul who gave him her fear, and got it all back at once
npcs.hail: *(The lot is wrong. You know it before you've crossed three tiles — the warmth is gone out of it, the whistling, the little court; the cairns of the slain that Sevrin left behind have gone cold and grey and ordinary, just heaps of grave-dust now, and in the middle of the silence a thin long blade stands point-down in the earth where someone drove it like a marker, and no one is cleaning it. And on the ground beside the log where the Last Word used to lounge, the young soul Sela sits with her arms wrapped around herself, not weeping — past weeping — just holding on, the way a soul holds on when the thing it set its whole weight against has been taken out from under it and it has not yet finished falling. She does not look up at you. She knows the sound of feet that aren't his.)* …he's gone. *(flat, hollow, a voice with the floor knocked out of it)* …i don't — i keep *saying* it, to make it — *'he's* *gone'* — and it *won't* go *in*, cold one. it just — it *bounces* *off.* *(she presses her face to her knees)* …you don't *understand.* *nobody* *understands.* he had it. he had — *(her voice cracks)* …he had *my* — i *gave* him — *(she can't finish, and starts over)* …i wasn't *afraid* *anymore*, cold one. for the *first* *time* since i *died*, i set it *down*, the *whole* *weight* of it, i *handed* it to *him* and he *took* it like it was *nothing* and i was *free* …and now he's — *(she looks up at last, and her eyes are the eyes of an animal that woke in a trap)* …and it's all come *back.* …*all* of it. *all* at *once.* …*everything* i *stopped* *carrying* …the *day* he *winked* at me … …it's *all* *back* on *me* now, cold one, *and* the *terror* of having *forgotten* how to *hold* it …*and* he's not *here* …*and* he's *never* …*(barely)* …*how* do you — …how does a soul *carry* …what it *gave* *away …*and* *forgot* …*how* to *lift?**

npcs.name: Coll
npcs.title: the one who watched with his eyes open, so someone would be left
npcs.hail: *(The old soul stands a little apart, by the deadtree, exactly where he stood when Sevrin was alive — but he is not grieving the way Sela grieves. He is grieving the way a man grieves who saw it coming, who has seen it coming a dozen times, who carried the foreknowledge so long that when the blow finally fell it landed not as shock but as the closing of a thing long open. He is steady. His eyes are wet and clear. He has, you understand at once, done this before — been the one left standing — and he is already, quietly, turning to the work of it.)* …you came *back.* *(a low, worn voice)* …i hoped you might. *(he looks at the blade standing in the earth)* …it happened the way they *always* happen, cold one. you'll *know*, if you *saw* — and i think you *did*, or you'd not have that *look.* *(a long breath)* …he cleared the *field.* *brilliant*, to the *last* — killed the *worthy* ones *first* and *fast*, *exactly* as a *master* *should.* and then he — *(his jaw works, but his voice holds)* …he *turned* to say something. *winked* at the *frightened* one. *'i've* *got* *this.'* …and there was *one* *left.* *(he closes his eyes, opens them)* …there is *always* …*one* *left.* *(he turns to you, and the grief in him has a terrible competence to it)* …the *court's* *shattered*, cold one. *Sela* most of *all* — she *handed* him her *whole* *fear* and he *took* it *into* the *nothing* with him and *left* her *holding* *air.* …and somebody has to be *the* *after* for her. *(he meets your eyes)* …i've *been* the *after* …more *times* than i can *count.* …but i'll *tell* you a *true* thing, cold one: …it is *lighter* …*so* *much* *lighter* …when there are *two.**

npcs.name: Tamsin
npcs.title: a soul of the court who will not believe the Last Word can die
npcs.hail: *(One soul of the broken court has not sat down, has not gone hollow, has not bowed under the grief — because she has not let the grief in. She is pacing, brisk, almost cheerful in a brittle, glassy way, tidying the cold lot with quick birdlike movements, and when she sees you her face lights with a terrible bright hope.)* …oh — *you.* you were *here* *earlier*, weren't you, you *talked* to him! *(she clasps her hands)* …good, *good*, then you *know* — you *know* what he *is.* *Returned*, cold one. *nine* *times.* …everyone's being so — *(she gestures at Sela, at Coll, with a flutter of exasperated fondness)* …so *dramatic*, so *grim*, sitting about *mourning* like it's — and i keep *telling* them, i *keep* — *(her smile is fixed)* …he's *Returned.* he's *died* *before.* *nine* *times*, cold one, and *every* *single* *time* he has *come* *back.* that's — that's the *whole* of *what* he *is*, the man who *comes* *back*, the *Last* *Word*, he *always* gets the — *(a quick, glassy laugh)* …so this is just — it's just *number* *ten*, isn't it. he's *off* in the *Fugue* somewhere, *sorting* himself out, and *any* *moment* he'll come *strolling* back *whistling* and find us all *weeping* and he'll *laugh* at us, he'll *say* *'oh* ye of *little* —' *(her voice climbs)* …so i'm *not* going to *cry*, cold one. i'm *not.* because crying would mean — it would *mean* i — *(the smile trembles)* …*tell* them. …*you* talked to him. …*tell* them he's *coming* *back.* …*he* *always* … …he *always* *comes* *back.* …*doesn't* …*he?**

variants.text: *(She looks up at you, and her face does something complicated and terrible — because she knows what you did, knows you are the reason she is sitting here grieving instead of lying in the grass unmade, and the gratitude and the guilt and the grief are all at war in her at once.)* …it was *you.* *(barely)* …you *made* him *promise.* to *watch* the *small* ones. i *heard* about it — the *court* *talked* of *nothing* *else*, the *cold* one who got the *Last* *Word* to *check* *corners* like a *recruit.* *(her voice shakes)* …and at the *end*, cold one — when the *nothing* in the *corner* *came* — he *saw* it. *because* of *you.* he *saw* it *coming*, a *half*-*second* *early* … …and he *used* that *half*-*second* …to *throw* *me* *clear.* *(she presses both hands to her mouth)* …he *died* *doing* *for* *me* …the *one* *thing* …he *told* me *once* …he *couldn't* *do* …in his *first* *life. …protect* the *small* one. *(she is shaking)* …i'm *alive*, cold one. i'm *alive* *because* you *made* him *look* at the *corners* …and *because* he *spent* the *half*-*second* it *bought* him …*on* *me*, instead of *himself.* …and i don't — *(she looks at you, wrecked)* …i don't *know* how to *hold* a *gift* …*this* *heavy.* …*how* do i — …how does a soul *carry* …*being* *saved* …*like* *that?**

*(She looks up, and she is grieving — deeply, truly — but she is not destroyed; she is sitting upright, and trembling, and *holding*, and you understand that the difference is the thing you said to her, the colder gift, the one she almost didn't take.)* …you *warned* me. *(quietly)* …you *sat* *here*, before, and you told me — *'take* some of the *fear* *back, while* there's still *someone* to *teach* you' — and i *hated* it, cold one, it was *such* a *cold* thing to *say* in the *warm* of his *certainty* … …but i *did* it. *(she draws a shaking breath)* …a *little.* i took a *little* of it *back.* started *carrying* my *own* *fear* *again*, just a *handful*, while he was — while there was still — *(her voice catches)* …and *then* he *fell*, cold one. and *all* the *rest* of it came *crashing* *back* … …but *not* *all* at *once.* …because i'd *already* — i was *already* *holding* *some.* …*(she looks at her own hands)* …i'm not — i'm not *destroyed*, cold one. i *thought* i would be. i thought if he *ever* — i'd just *stop*, *shatter*, *blow* *away.* …but i'm *here.* …*standing* it. …*barely.* …because *you* *made* me *keep* …the *one* *muscle* …that was *actually* *mine.* …*how* …*how* did you *know?**

*(She does not look up. She is too far down for that.)* …did you know him. *(flat, hollow)* …it doesn't *matter.* nobody *knew* him, not *really* — we just *stood* near him and let his *sureness* get *on* us like *warmth* off a *fire*, and called it *knowing.* *(she wraps tighter around herself)* …i *gave* him my *fear*, cold one. *all* of it. the *whole* *weight* i'd carried since i *died.* he *took* it with a *wink* and i was *free* and i *followed* him *glad* and i *never* — not *once* — let myself *think* …what would *happen* …to the *one* who *gave* her *fear *away* …if the *one* who *held* it …could *fall.* *(a long, awful silence)* …and now it's *back.* *all* of it. *all* at *once.* and i don't — *(barely)* …i *forgot* *how*, cold one. that's the — that's the *thing* nobody *warned* me. you *hand* it *away* …and your *arms* *forget* …how to *lift* it. …and *now* it's *back* in *arms* …that *don't* *remember* …*how* to *hold.**

nodes.speaker: Sela
nodes.text: *(She wipes her face with the heel of her hand, a small useless gesture, and lets you stay, because being alone in it is worse.)* …*ask*, if you've things to *ask.* *(a hollow breath)* …i'm *poor* *company* now — i was *poor* *company* *before*, all i could do was *sing* his *praises*, and now i can't even — *(she trails off)* …*say* what you *came* to *say*, cold one. …i *haven't* the *strength* …to *guess* at it.

choices.text: *(read the one who got her fear back all at once — past the wreckage, to whether there's a floor under her anywhere)* "Let me look at you, Sela. Properly. Past the falling."
"Tell me what it's like, Sela. Not the grief — the fear. The weight you handed him, coming back. I want to understand exactly what you're holding."

*(the one who knows the cruelest truth and the only mercy in it: that the fear coming back is not a punishment but the return of the one thing that was always hers — and that carrying it herself is how she stops needing to be saved)* "Sela — I'm going to tell you the hardest true thing I know, and I need you to hear it past the grief, because it's the only thing that will get you off this ground. The fear coming back isn't a punishment. It isn't the world being cruel to a soul that already lost everything. It's the return of the one thing that was always, only, *yours.* You handed Sevrin your safety, and he gave it back tenfold — but you also handed him your *fear*, and that was never his to keep, because a soul's fear is not a burden someone carries *for* you. It's the part of you that's still awake. It's the part that watches the corners, that keeps you alive in the grey, that knows the dark is real and refuses to pretend otherwise. When you gave it to him, you didn't get free — you went to *sleep*, safe and lovely and asleep, and a sleeping soul in this country is a soul halfway to dissolved. The Wall doesn't need to come for the souls who've handed their fear away and stopped watching. They drift to it on their own. So this — this terrible weight crashing back into arms that forgot how to lift — this is you *waking up.* It is the worst feeling in the worlds and it is the sound of you becoming a whole soul again, one who can stand her own dark, who doesn't need a wink from a stronger creature to survive the night. He can't carry it for you anymore. That's not the tragedy, Sela. That's the *inheritance.* He gave you back the fear so you could learn, at last, the thing he never could — not how to never lose anyone, but how to *keep standing* after you do. Lift it. It's heavy because it's yours. And a soul carrying its own fear, in the teeth of the grey, having lost the one who held it — that is the bravest thing this whole country has ever seen, and it is the one thing the dark can never, ever take, because it's already in your hands."

"There's an old soul by the deadtree — Coll — who's grieved this before and is still standing. And he asked after you. Will you let him be the after with you, Sela? You don't have to carry the first hour alone."
"Stay on the ground a while longer, Sela. I'll come back. You don't have to stand up the moment I tell you to."

variants.text: *(Your sense finds — my sense, Returned; no more pretending — and i return to this one with interest, for she is the rarest thing: a soul the nothing nearly had, clawing back awake. Look at what the falling uncovered:)* *(She lets you look, too emptied to resist, and you move your cold past the wreckage of her to find whether anything in her still holds.)* *(And your sense finds, under the falling, a thing that surprises you: a floor. Not a strong one — cracked, deep down, half-forgotten — but *there.* Your sense reads the soul that survived the grey *alone*, before Sevrin, weeks of it, the animal terror with no protector and no wink and no warm certainty to lean on — and reads that she did survive it, that the surviving took a real strength, a strength she has so completely handed away and filed under *the bad time before he came* that she no longer believes she ever had it. And your sense reads the cruelty of the present moment and the mercy hidden inside it at once: that the fear crashing back into her is unbearable, yes, but it is crashing back into a soul that has *held it before* — that the muscle is not gone, only sleeping, atrophied under nine months of being carried, and that it is, even now, in the very act of waking, beginning — clumsily, agonizingly — to *lift.* Your sense reads that Sela believes she is shattering. Your sense reads that she is, in fact, doing the opposite — that this is what it looks like, from the inside, when a soul that outsourced itself is forced to become whole again, and that the forcing is brutal but the wholeness is real. And your sense reads the one thing she most needs and least believes: that she is not, has never been, the helpless creature the wink let her become; that the soul who crossed the grey alone is still in there, under the grief, and remembers — if she will let it — exactly how to stand.)* *(…a floor under the wreckage, the old strength waking — and i will be honest, even with you, even now: i would rather she had stayed asleep and drifted to me, for that is the easier harvest the Wall depends on. That she wakes is a defiance of the law that wanted her dreaming toward the nothing. A soul who has seen Sela claw back cannot, in conscience, leave the law that nearly took her standing. Her waking is a small warm thing. The law is the wrong. You know whose grief you see her by — and what i would have you do to it.)*

*(She lets you look, too emptied to resist, and you move your cold past the wreckage of her to find whether anything in her still holds.)* *(And your sense finds, under the falling, a thing that surprises you: a floor. Not a strong one — cracked, deep down, half-forgotten — but *there.* Your sense reads the soul that survived the grey *alone*, before Sevrin, weeks of it, the animal terror with no protector and no wink and no warm certainty to lean on — and reads that she did survive it, that the surviving took a real strength, a strength she has so completely handed away and filed under *the bad time before he came* that she no longer believes she ever had it. And your sense reads the cruelty of the present moment and the mercy hidden inside it at once: that the fear crashing back into her is unbearable, yes, but it is crashing back into a soul that has *held it before* — that the muscle is not gone, only sleeping, atrophied under nine months of being carried, and that it is, even now, in the very act of waking, beginning — clumsily, agonizingly — to *lift.* Your sense reads that Sela believes she is shattering. Your sense reads that she is, in fact, doing the opposite — that this is what it looks like, from the inside, when a soul that outsourced itself is forced to become whole again, and that the forcing is brutal but the wholeness is real. And your sense reads the one thing she most needs and least believes: that she is not, has never been, the helpless creature the wink let her become; that the soul who crossed the grey alone is still in there, under the grief, and remembers — if she will let it — exactly how to stand.)*

nodes.speaker: Sela
nodes.text: *(She is quiet a moment, gathering it, and when she speaks it is with the awful precision of someone describing a wound while it's still open.)* …what's it *like.* *(a breath)* …you know how i *told* you — *before* — what it was like when he *lifted* it *off?* the *arms* floating *up*, *light*, like they might *drift* off my *body?* *(she nods to herself)* …it's *that.* …*backwards.* …*all* at *once.* *(she wraps her arms tighter)* …it didn't come back *slow*, cold one. it didn't *ease* in. the *moment* he — the *moment* i *understood* he was *gone* — it *all* came *down* at *once*, the *whole* *weight*, *months* of it, *everything* he'd been *holding* …*slammed* back into *arms* that had gone *soft* and *light* and *forgotten* … *(her voice shakes)* …and it's not *just* the *weight*, cold one. it's the — it's the *shame* of how *much* it *is.* i'd *forgotten* it was *this* *heavy.* i'd *forgotten* i used to *carry* *this*, *every* *moment*, *before* him — and now i *can't* *believe* i *ever* — and i can't believe i *gave* it to *someone* *else* and called it *freedom* … *(she looks at you)* …it *frightens* me, cold one. the *fear* *itself* *frightens* me now, because it's *bigger* than i *remember* …and i'm *weaker* than i *was* …and the *one* who *made* it *bearable* …is the *reason* …it's *unbearable.**

nodes.speaker: Sela
nodes.text: *(She listens — and somewhere in the middle of it she stops holding herself quite so tightly, and her hands, which have been clamped around her own arms, loosen, and turn, slowly, palm-up, as though testing a weight — and by the end the young soul is weeping, but it is a different weeping, not the bottomless hollow kind but the kind that has somewhere to go.)* …*'it's heavy because it's yours.'* *(barely)* …cold one. …i — *(she looks at her open hands)* …i've been *holding* it like a — like a *punishment.* like the *world* *took* him *and* *gave* me *back* the *fear* to — to *finish* me. …and you're — *(a wet, broken breath)* …you're saying it's the *opposite.* that the fear coming back is — is *me.* …*waking* *up.* …*(she presses her palms together, then apart, testing)* …i *did* cross the grey *alone*, cold one. …*before* him. i *did.* i'd *forgotten* — i'd *filed* it under *the* *bad* *time* — but i *crossed* it, *weeks* of it, *no* *wink*, *no* one, just — just *me*, and my *fear*, and i *kept* *standing.* *(she lifts her head)* …i *forgot* i *could* *do* that. …he *let* me *forget.* …not *cruel* — he *thought* he was being *kind*, holding it for me — but he *let* me *forget* …the *one* *thing* …that was *only* *mine.* *(and slowly, agonizingly, she gets her feet under her, and stands — shaking, streaming, but standing)* …*all* *right.* …*all* *right*, cold one. …it's *mine.* …it's *heavy* because it's *mine.* …and i *carried* it *before* …and i'll — *(she sways, steadies)* …i'll *carry* it *now.* …*myself.* …and the *Wall* — *(her voice finds an edge it never had)* …the *Wall* that *waits* for the *souls* who *handed* their *fear* *away* and *stopped* *watching* …*(she wipes her face)* …it can *wait.* …i'm *awake* *now.* …*thank* you. …it's the *coldest* *gift* …*twice* now …you've *given* me a *colder* *gift* than the *wink.* …and *this* one — *(barely, standing)* …*this* one …is the *only* *reason* …i'm on my *feet.**

nodes.speaker: Sela
nodes.text: *(She follows your glance to the old soul by the deadtree, and something in her face flickers — wary, but not closed.)* …*Coll.* *(a hollow breath)* …i *avoided* him, cold one. the *whole* *time.* we *all* did — the *court.* because he was the — he was the *cloud.* the *one* who *wouldn't* *pretend.* the one who *looked* at *Sevrin* and saw a — a *funeral* *coming*, and we *hated* him for it, because we'd *handed* away our *fear* and he *wouldn't* *let* us *forget* it was *owed.* *(her voice cracks)* …and he was — he was *right*, wasn't he. the *whole* *time.* the *cloud* was *right* and the *sunshine* was the *lie.* *(she looks at Coll, and there is something like shame, and something like relief)* …and now he's the *only* one *still* *standing.* …because he *never* *handed* it *away.* …he *kept* his *fear* …the *whole* *time* …so he'd *have* it *now.* *(barely)* …*all* *right*, cold one. …i'll — …i'll *go* to *him.* …if anyone *knows* …how to *carry* this *first* *hour* …it's the *soul* who's *carried* it …*a* *dozen* *times* …*alone.* …and *maybe* — *(a small, wrecked breath)* …maybe it's *lighter* …with *two.* …he'd *know.* …*Coll* …*would* *know.**

nodes.speaker: Sela
nodes.text: *(She nods, a small grateful thing, and does not let go of herself, but does not fall any further either.)* …*thank* you. …for not — *(a hollow breath)* …for not telling me to *stand* *up.* …everyone *wants* me to *stand* *up.* to *be* *better.* to *grieve* *tidy* and *quick* so they don't have to — *(she shakes her head)* …*you* just *let* me be *on* the *ground.* …that's — *(barely)* …that's the *kindest* thing anyone's *done* …since the *wink.* …go on, cold one. …and — *(she hesitates)* …*Tamsin.* the *bright* one, *pacing.* …*mind* her, would you. …she's *worse* than me, cold one, though she *looks* *better.* …*i'm* on the *ground* …because i *know* he's *gone.* …*she's* still *up* …because she *won't* *let* it *be* *true.* …and the *ground* …*(softly)* …the *ground's* the *safer* *place* …of the *two.**

variants.text: *(He turns as you approach, and there is grief in him, deep and real — but there is also something the other mourners do not have: readiness. He spoke with you before the fall, and the two of you saw it coming together, and that shared sight is, right now, the only steady thing in the lot.)* …*two* of us. *(quietly)* …i *told* you, before — *'if* the *day* *comes* that the *Last* *Word* *can't*, *find* me; *we'll* *carry* *Sela* *together.'* *(he looks at the blade in the earth)* …well. …the *day* *came*, cold one. it *always* *comes.* …but for the *first* *time* in *more* *lifetimes* than i can *count* …*(his voice catches, holds)* …i am *not* the *only* one *left* *standing* with my *eyes* *open.* …you *believed* me. when it *mattered.* *before.* …and so now, in the *after* …i am not *alone* in the *one* *job* that *outlives* the *legend.* …*(he meets your eyes, and the gratitude in them is enormous and old)* …you've *no* *idea*, cold one …what it is …to *grieve* the *bright* one …*beside* someone …instead of *behind* them.*

*(He turns as you approach, steady, clear-eyed, already at the work of the after — but there is a weariness in him deeper than the grief, the weariness of a soul doing a familiar thing alone for the umpteenth time.)* …you came *back.* *(a worn breath)* …most don't, you know. the *court* *scatters*, after. *grief*-*blind.* they *find* a *new* *bright* one and *never* *connect* the *deaths*, and so they *learn* *nothing*, and it *happens* *again*, *exactly* the *same*, *somewhere* *else.* *(he looks at the standing blade)* …i'm the *only* one who *stays.* the *only* one who *counts.* …*(he sighs, ancient)* …i *wish* you'd — *(he stops himself)* …*no.* no use *wishing.* …you're *here* *now*, in the *after*, and that's *more* than *most* *manage.* …*sit* with an old *soul*, cold one. …there's *work* in *grief* …and it's *lighter* *shared.*

nodes.speaker: Coll
nodes.text: *(He lowers himself onto the cold log where Sevrin used to lounge — a deliberate thing, you sense, claiming the warm seat for the cold work — and folds his old hands.)* …*ask*, cold one. *(a steady breath)* …i'm *better* *company* than *Sela*, just *now* — not because i *grieve* *less*, but because i've *grieved* *this* *exact* *shape* *before*, and a *grief* you've *carried* *before* …*sits* *different.* *lighter* on the *back*, even when it's *just* as *heavy* in the *heart.* …*ask.*

choices.text: *(read the soul who has been the after a dozen times — past the steadiness, to what it costs to be the one who always survives)* "Let me look at you, Coll. At what it's cost — being the one who's always left."
"You said it: you saw it coming, lifetimes of it. So tell me honestly, Coll — does knowing make it easier, or worse? To grieve a death you predicted?"

*(the one who finally honors the thing Coll is — not the prophet of doom the courts hate, but the keeper of the dead's last dignity: the one who refuses to let the fallen be forgotten the way the Wall forgets)* "Coll — I want to name what you actually are, because the courts never have, and you've earned it before the end. They think you're the cloud. The bitter old man who can't bear the bright ones, who shows up to spoil the warmth with his funerals-that-haven't-happened. But that's not what you are at all. You're the only soul in this whole grey country who refuses to let the magnificent be *forgotten.* Every court scatters when its legend falls — grief-blind, they run, they find a new bright one, and the dead one is gone, uncounted, unremembered, dissolved out of the story as completely as if the Wall had taken him. And the Wall *wants* that. That's exactly how it erases souls — not just the unloved nobodies, but the legends too: let the grief scatter the witnesses, and even a soul of eleven lifetimes vanishes without a single mind left holding the shape of him. But you stay. You count. You lay the deaths side by side and you *remember* — Haldane, and the ones before Haldane, and now Sevrin — you keep the only ledger of the fallen bright that exists, and you do it alone, lifetime after lifetime, because someone has to be the soul the dead can be remembered *by.* That's not morbidity, Coll. That's the same war I'm walking toward a Wall to fight, fought at the scale of one grieving man. You are a soul who has decided that the magnificent will not be erased on your watch — that even the legends who wouldn't listen to you, who called you a cloud, who died exactly as you said they would — *especially* those — will have, at the last, one soul who saw them whole, saw them fall, and refused to forget. You don't follow the bright ones to gloat. You follow them so that when they go, they are not *un-marked.* You're not the cloud, Coll. You're the keeper of their names. And there is no holier work in this whole country than that."
"Sela's on the ground and Tamsin won't come down off the ceiling. You've done this a dozen times, Coll. What does the after actually need? Tell me how to help."
"Keep the warm seat, Coll. I'll move through the rest of the wreckage and come back. I'm glad it's you who's still standing."

variants.text: *(Your sense finds — my sense, Returned; the eyes have been mine all along and you have made your peace with it — and this old griever i look at almost tenderly, for he and i keep the same trade: holding the shape of the lost when the world lets them go. Look:)* *(He lets you look, patient as stone, and you move your cold past the competent grief to the soul that has had to build it.)* *(And your sense finds the cost, where it always hides in souls like this — not in the grieving, which he does cleanly, but in the *accumulation* of it. Your sense reads a soul that is, in the most literal sense, made of other people's funerals — a steady ordinary man who has attached himself, lifetime after lifetime, to bright unkillable creatures precisely *because* he is not one, and watched every single one die, and survived every single death, and carried each loss forward into the next life like stones added to a pack that is never set down. And your sense reads the specific loneliness of his position: that he cannot grieve *with* anyone, because the courts always scatter and he is always the only one who stays; that he has stood at the falling of a dozen legends and been, each time, the sole clear-eyed witness, the one who saw and counted and remembered while everyone around him ran blind; and that this has made him wise and steady and unbreakable and *unbearably* alone — a soul who knows more about loss than any creature in the grey and has had no one, not once, to share the knowing with. And your sense reads the thing underneath even that, the wound he would never name: that some small buried part of Coll wonders whether he is *cursed* — whether an ordinary soul who outlives every magnificent one he loves is being punished, or worse, whether his ordinariness is *why* he survives, whether the loop spares him precisely because he is not bright enough to be worth taking, and leaves him alive only to watch, forever, the bright ones it does want, fall. And your sense reads the grace he made of it anyway: that he took that curse, that endless lonely surviving, and turned it into a *vocation* — decided that if he was doomed to always be the one left, he would be the *best* one left, the one who counted, who remembered, who could gather the shattered and say *I know, I saw, come* — and that this decision is the only thing that has kept ten lifetimes of accumulated grief from crushing him flat. He is a soul who was handed nothing but loss and made himself, out of it, the keeper of the lost.)* *(…a soul handed nothing but loss and made, of it, the keeper of the lost — left to survive every legend he loved because the loop saw no profit in taking him. The gods' wheel made him this. I would unmake the wheel and let him, at last, set the dead down. A soul who has seen what it costs to be the after cannot, in conscience, leave the thing that demands an after standing. His keeping is small and warm. The wheel is the wrong. You know whose eyes you grieve by now — and what i would have you do with the grief.)*

*(He lets you look, patient as stone, and you move your cold past the competent grief to the soul that has had to build it.)* *(And your sense finds the cost, where it always hides in souls like this — not in the grieving, which he does cleanly, but in the *accumulation* of it. Your sense reads a soul that is, in the most literal sense, made of other people's funerals — a steady ordinary man who has attached himself, lifetime after lifetime, to bright unkillable creatures precisely *because* he is not one, and watched every single one die, and survived every single death, and carried each loss forward into the next life like stones added to a pack that is never set down. And your sense reads the specific loneliness of his position: that he cannot grieve *with* anyone, because the courts always scatter and he is always the only one who stays; that he has stood at the falling of a dozen legends and been, each time, the sole clear-eyed witness, the one who saw and counted and remembered while everyone around him ran blind; and that this has made him wise and steady and unbreakable and *unbearably* alone — a soul who knows more about loss than any creature in the grey and has had no one, not once, to share the knowing with. And your sense reads the thing underneath even that, the wound he would never name: that some small buried part of Coll wonders whether he is *cursed* — whether an ordinary soul who outlives every magnificent one he loves is being punished, or worse, whether his ordinariness is *why* he survives, whether the loop spares him precisely because he is not bright enough to be worth taking, and leaves him alive only to watch, forever, the bright ones it does want, fall. And your sense reads the grace he made of it anyway: that he took that curse, that endless lonely surviving, and turned it into a *vocation* — decided that if he was doomed to always be the one left, he would be the *best* one left, the one who counted, who remembered, who could gather the shattered and say *I know, I saw, come* — and that this decision is the only thing that has kept ten lifetimes of accumulated grief from crushing him flat. He is a soul who was handed nothing but loss and made himself, out of it, the keeper of the lost.)*

nodes.speaker: Coll
nodes.text: *(He is quiet a long moment, looking at the blade standing in the earth, and when he answers it is the most honest thing he has said.)* …does *knowing* make it *easier.* *(a slow breath)* …*both*, cold one. that's the *true* *answer*, and it's a *cruel* one. *(he folds his hands)* …it makes it *easier* …because i am *never* *blindsided.* i do not have *Sela's* *grief* — the *grief* that *also* *carries* the *shock*, the *betrayal*, the *'how* *could* *this* *happen'* — because i *always* *knew* *how* it could *happen*, i *watched* it *coming*, i was *braced.* *(his jaw works)* …but it makes it *worse*, cold one …in a way i've *never* *told* a *living* *soul …because* i have to *carry* the *months* *before.* *(barely)* …*Sela* got to be *happy*, you *see.* *moonstruck.* *free.* she *handed* away her *fear* and *lived* in the *warm* of his *certainty* and *did* *not* *know.* …and i — *(his voice roughens)* …i *stood* in that *same* *warm*, cold one, that *same* *court*, and i *could* *not* *enjoy* a *moment* of it, because i *knew* what was *coming*, i *always* *know*, and so i *grieve* them *while* they're *still* *alive.* …i *grieved* *Sevrin* …*every* *day* …i *watched* him *whistle.* *(he looks at you)* …*that's* the *cost* of the *clear* *eye*, cold one. …the *blindsided* grieve *hard* …but *only* *after.* …i grieve *soft* …but i grieve the *whole* *time.* …i have *never*, in a *dozen* *lifetimes* …got to *simply* *love* …a *bright* *thing* …*without* …already …*mourning* it.*

nodes.speaker: Coll
nodes.text: *(He listens — and the old steady soul who has been the cloud at a dozen funerals, who has carried his vocation alone for lifetimes, who has never once been *seen* for what he is, begins, silently, to weep, and does not wipe it away, because some part of him has waited an age for exactly these words and will not now hurry past them.)* …*'the keeper of their names.'* *(barely)* …cold one. …*(his old hands tremble in his lap)* …do you know what they *call* me. the *courts.* the *bright* ones. …*the* *cloud.* *the* *raven.* *the* *bitter* *old* *man* who *can't* *bear* to see *anyone* *happy.* …and i *let* them, cold one, because the *truth* — *(his voice breaks)* …the *truth* was *too* *long* to *say* and *too* *strange* to *believe*, and i had *no* *words* for it, so i just — i *bore* the *names* they *gave* me …and went on *counting* …*alone.* *(he looks at you, wet, undone)* …and *you've* — in *one* — you've *named* it. *'you* *follow* them so that when they *go*, they are *not* *un-marked.'* …*yes.* …*gods*, cold one, *yes.* …that's the *whole* of it, the *thing* i've *done* for *lifetimes* and *never* *once* been able to *say*: …i *cannot* *save* them. …i *learned* that *long* *ago.* …but i can *refuse* …to let them *vanish.* …i can be the *one* *mind* …that *holds* the *shape* of *them* …after the *court* *scatters* …and the *Wall* *reaches* up …to *take* even the *legends* …*un-remembered.* *(he straightens, and the grief has a fierce new spine in it)* …*Haldane.* the *Unbroken* *Blade.* *eleven* *lifetimes.* …*i* *remember* him, cold one. when *no* one *else* in *all* the *worlds* *does* …*i* *do.* …and *Sevrin* — *(he looks at the standing blade)* …the *Last* *Word.* *nine* *lifetimes.* …*winked* at a *frightened* *girl* and *died* *saving* her. …*i* will *remember* *that.* …and when *i* *finally* *fall*, cold one — the *ordinary* *soul* who *outlived* them *all* — *(barely)* …i had *feared* there'd be *no* one to *remember* *me.* …the *keeper* …*un-kept.* …but *now* — *(he meets your eyes)* …*now* there's *you.* …a *cold* one …who *saw* …what i *am.* …*you'll* *remember* …*the* *one* …who *remembered* …*won't* *you.* …*that's* — *(he can hardly speak)* …*that's* *all* …a *keeper* …*ever* *wanted.* …to be, at the *last* …*kept.**

nodes.speaker: Coll
nodes.text: *(He nods, grateful for the practical turn — a soul who has learned that grief is survived in tasks, not feelings.)* …*good.* *good* question, cold one. most don't *ask* — they *flail*, or they *flee.* *(he gathers himself)* …here's what the *after* *needs.* *(he counts it on old fingers)* …*one.* *Sela* needs to be *let* *be* *on* the *ground.* don't *haul* her *up.* don't tell her she's *strong*, or that he's *at* *peace*, or any of the *tidy* *lies.* just — *be* *near.* let the *falling* *finish.* a soul can't *stand* *back* *up* until it's *done* *falling* *down*, and *rushing* it only *teaches* it to *fall* *in* *secret.* *(a second finger)* …*two.* *Tamsin.* …*Tamsin's* the *hard* one, cold one — *harder* than *Sela*, though she *looks* the *opposite.* *Sela's* *grief* is *honest.* *Tamsin's* …*Tamsin* has *not* *let* it *in* *yet.* she's *up* on the *ceiling*, *insisting* he'll *come* *back* — and she's *not* *wrong* to, *exactly*, that's the *cruelty* of it, he's *Returned*, he *has* *always* come *back* …*before.* *(his jaw tightens)* …but the *unmaking*, cold one — the *Wall* on the *claw* — …*that's* the *one* *death* a *Returned* *doesn't* *walk* *back* *from.* …*Tamsin* doesn't *know* that. or won't. and *someone* …*gently* …*so* *gently* …has to be the one to *take* her *down* off the *ceiling* …and *teach* her …the *difference* …between a *death* …and an *unmaking.* *(he looks at you)* …it'll *break* her, cold one. …*properly.* …but the *breaking* …is the only *door* …to the *grieving.* …and a soul that *never* *grieves* …just *waits* …*forever* …at a *door* …*no* one's *coming* *through.**

nodes.speaker: Coll
nodes.text: *(He inclines his head, and for the first time something almost like peace moves under the ancient grief.)* …go on, cold one. …*mind* *Tamsin*, if you've the *heart* for it — she's the *one* who *needs* it *most* and *wants* it *least.* *(he settles on the warm seat, keeping the cold work)* …and cold one — *(quietly, to your back)* …*thank* you. …for *coming* *back* to the *after.* …most souls *only* *want* the *legend.* the *whistling*, the *wink*, the *warm.* …*nobody* …*comes* *back* …for the *cold* *lot* …and the *grieving* …and the *old* *man* …who *counts* *the* *dead.* *(a worn breath)* …*you* did. …and so *whatever* *else* *happens* …on that *road* of *yours* …*know* this, from a soul who's *seen* a *dozen* *legends* *fall*: …the ones who *come* *back* *for* the *after* …*(barely)* …*those* are the ones …who *change* …how the *story* …*ends.**

conversations.title: Tamsin, Who Waits at the Door

variants.text: *(She is sitting now, finally, on the cold ground — the brittle brightness gone out of her, replaced by something rawer and truer and infinitely heavier, and infinitely better. She is weeping, openly, the way a soul weeps when it has stopped fighting the thing it was fighting, and when she looks up at you there is no glassy hope left, only grief, and a terrible exhausted gratitude.)* …you *came* *back.* *(a wet breath)* …i'm — *(she gestures at her own streaming face, a small helpless laugh)* …*look* at me. i'm *crying.* i said i *wouldn't.* i said it would *mean* he was — *(she stops, and makes herself say it)* …he's *gone*, cold one. …*truly* *gone.* the *kind* that *doesn't* *come* *back.* …you *helped* me — *(her voice breaks)* …you helped me *understand* the *difference.* …and it's *worse*, cold one. it's *so* much *worse* than the *waiting* — the *waiting* was *almost* *comfortable*, i could've *waited* *forever* — …but it's *true.* …and i think — *(barely)* …i think *true* and *grieving* …is *better* …than *false* and *waiting* …at a *door* …*no* one's *coming* *through.* …*thank* you …for *taking* me *down* …off the *ceiling.* …*even* though …it *broke* me.*

*(She is still pacing, still bright, still tidying the cold lot with those quick brittle movements, and she rounds on you with that fixed, glassy, desperate hope.)* …*there* you *are* — *good*, you're *back*, did you — *(she searches your face)* …have you *seen* him? out *there*, in the *Fugue*, *sorting* himself — no? …*no*, *not* *yet*, but *soon*, these *things* take — *(she laughs, too high)* …*nine* *times*, cold one. he's done this *nine* *times.* you don't just — a soul like *Sevrin* doesn't just *stop.* …*ask* me whatever you like, but i'll *tell* you *now*, i'll not *hear* a *word* of the *grim* *talk*, not from *you* *too*, i've *had* *enough* of it from *Coll* and his — his *raven's* *croak* — …*Sevrin's* *coming* *back.* …*say* you *believe* it. …*please.* …it's so much — *(her smile shakes)* …it's so much *easier* to *bear* …with *someone* *believing* it *too.*

nodes.speaker: Tamsin
nodes.text: *(She perches on the edge of the log, unable to be still, hands fluttering, that bright brittle hope held up between you like a shield.)* …go *on*, then. *(a quick smile)* …but *kindly*, cold one. i'm — i'll *own* it — i'm *holding* on by a *thread* here, and the *thread* is *'he* *comes* *back*,' and if you *pull* it … *(the smile wavers)* …well. …just — be *kind.* …*ask*.

choices.text: *(read the one who won't come down off the ceiling — past the brightness, to what the denial is protecting her from)* "Hold still a moment, Tamsin — if you can. Let me look at you."
"You keep saying he comes back. Nine times. Tell me about those, Tamsin — the other times he died. What was it like, waiting, and being right?"

*(the hardest mercy in the lot — gently, so gently, take her down off the ceiling: teach her the difference between a death and an unmaking, and that waiting forever at a door no one's coming through is its own slow dissolving)* "Tamsin — I'm going to be kind, exactly as you asked, but kindness isn't always softness, and the kindest thing I have is also the hardest, so take my hands while I say it. You're right about something, and that's what makes this so cruel. He *did* come back. Nine times. He is Returned, and a Returned soul that dies the ordinary way — cut down, worn out, killed — *does* come back; you've watched it; you're not foolish to believe it. But there's a thing the court was spared and you weren't, because you loved him most: there are two ways for a soul like us to end, and only one of them is death. Death, we walk back from. But the thing on that claw, Tamsin — the grey on the small one in the corner — that wasn't a blade. It was the Wall's own touch. *Unmaking.* And unmaking isn't a death a Returned walks back from, because it doesn't send the soul to the Fugue to be sorted and pulled back. It *unwrites* it. There's no Sevrin in the grey sorting himself out, Tamsin. There's no door he's about to come whistling through. That's the cruelty the unmaking does that a clean death never could — it doesn't just take the person. It takes the *coming back.* And I know — I *know* — that the waiting feels like the last thread holding you to him, that to stop waiting feels like killing him a second time, like giving up, like betraying the one soul who never gave up on anything. But waiting at a door no one is ever going to come through isn't faith, Tamsin. It's a slow dissolving of your own. You'll stand there, bright and brittle and certain, while everything in you that could grieve and heal and someday be warm again just… wears away, unspent, at a threshold that stays shut. He doesn't need you to wait. He's past needing anything. What's left is *you* — and you are allowed to come down off the ceiling, and put your feet on the cold ground next to Sela, and grieve him the way you only grieve what you've finally let yourself *lose.* It will break you. I won't pretend it won't. But the breaking is the only door that *does* open — the one that lets the grief in, and the grief, terrible as it is, is the last true thing he left you. Let it in, Tamsin. Stop waiting for him to come back. Start carrying him instead."
"Stay up on your thread a while longer, Tamsin. I'll come back. I won't pull it before you can bear to let go."

variants.text: *(Your sense finds — my sense, Returned; we are long past the pretending — and i look at this one with a pity sharp as my own grief, for she has built her whole notion of a survivable world on a dead man, and the building is about to come down. Look:)* *(She goes still for half a heartbeat — the longest she's managed — and you move your cold past the glassy brightness to the thing it's built to keep out.)* *(And your sense finds, behind the manic hope, pure terror — not of the grief itself, but of a specific belief the grief would force her to face: that Tamsin, of all the court, gave Sevrin not just her fear but her whole sense that the worlds were *survivable.* Your sense reads a soul that was, in life and in death, a worrier — a frightened, anxious creature for whom the grey afterlife was simply unbearable, a place of endless dread — until Sevrin, and the discovery that if she stayed near him, near the man who could not lose, the dread switched *off.* And your sense reads what his certainty became for her: not comfort, but *cosmology* — proof that the universe was not, after all, a place where the worst always happens, because here was a soul the worst could not touch. And your sense reads, with a chill, why his death is so much more dangerous to her than to Sela: that if Sevrin can fall — if even the Last Word, the unbeatable, the one fixed point — then Tamsin's entire model of a survivable cosmos falls with him, and she is returned not just to grief but to the original terror, the conviction that nothing is safe and the worst always comes, *confirmed*, made absolute, by the fall of the one soul who disproved it. And your sense reads that this is why she will not let it in: not because she can't believe he's gone, but because believing it means believing the thing underneath, the thing her whole anxious soul cannot survive — that there is no fixed point, no unbeatable one, no proof the worst won't come, that the worst *did* come, for the best of them, and so can come for anyone, anytime, and there is no Sevrin left to stand between her and that knowledge. Your sense reads a soul clinging to a dead man not out of love alone, but because he was the last evidence she had that existence was bearable — and that taking her down off the ceiling will mean catching not just her grief, but the whole frightened soul beneath it, falling.)* *(…a frightened soul who made one bright certainty her cosmology, and now must learn the loop spares no one — which is the truth the Wall and the gods would rather she never face, that the worst comes for the best and there is no fixed point at all. I would make a world where that is no longer true, by unmaking the law that makes it true. A soul who has seen Tamsin's terror cannot, in conscience, leave the thing that justifies it standing. Taking her down gently is small and warm. The thing that proves her right is the wrong. You know whose eyes you weep for her by — and what i would have you do.)*

*(She goes still for half a heartbeat — the longest she's managed — and you move your cold past the glassy brightness to the thing it's built to keep out.)* *(And your sense finds, behind the manic hope, pure terror — not of the grief itself, but of a specific belief the grief would force her to face: that Tamsin, of all the court, gave Sevrin not just her fear but her whole sense that the worlds were *survivable.* Your sense reads a soul that was, in life and in death, a worrier — a frightened, anxious creature for whom the grey afterlife was simply unbearable, a place of endless dread — until Sevrin, and the discovery that if she stayed near him, near the man who could not lose, the dread switched *off.* And your sense reads what his certainty became for her: not comfort, but *cosmology* — proof that the universe was not, after all, a place where the worst always happens, because here was a soul the worst could not touch. And your sense reads, with a chill, why his death is so much more dangerous to her than to Sela: that if Sevrin can fall — if even the Last Word, the unbeatable, the one fixed point — then Tamsin's entire model of a survivable cosmos falls with him, and she is returned not just to grief but to the original terror, the conviction that nothing is safe and the worst always comes, *confirmed*, made absolute, by the fall of the one soul who disproved it. And your sense reads that this is why she will not let it in: not because she can't believe he's gone, but because believing it means believing the thing underneath, the thing her whole anxious soul cannot survive — that there is no fixed point, no unbeatable one, no proof the worst won't come, that the worst *did* come, for the best of them, and so can come for anyone, anytime, and there is no Sevrin left to stand between her and that knowledge. Your sense reads a soul clinging to a dead man not out of love alone, but because he was the last evidence she had that existence was bearable — and that taking her down off the ceiling will mean catching not just her grief, but the whole frightened soul beneath it, falling.)*

nodes.speaker: Tamsin
nodes.text: *(She brightens, eager, seizing the chance to build her case.)* …*oh* — *yes*, the *other* times, *let* me — *(she counts on fluttering fingers)* …well, i only *saw* the *last* *two*, mind, i've *only* *followed* him *two* *lifetimes* — but he *told* us about the *others*, the *court* *loved* the *stories* — *(she smiles)* …the *third* *time*, he *died* in a *wraith*-*nest*, *torn* *apart*, *gone* a *whole* *month* — and he came *back.* the *sixth*, a *Convergence* *took* him, *swallowed* him *whole*, and the *court* *mourned* for — and he came *strolling* *back* *whistling*, *cross* that we'd *given* up on him. *(her smile is feverish now)* …*every* *time*, cold one. *every* *single* *time*, someone said *'this* is *it*, he's *truly* *gone'* …and *every* *time* they were *wrong*, and he *came* *back*, and he *never* *let* them *forget* they'd *doubted* him. …*(her voice climbs)* …so *why* — *why* is *this* *time* *different?* why does *Coll* keep *saying* — *(she falters, just slightly, the first real crack)* …he keeps saying a *word.* *'unmaking.'* …and i don't — i *won't* — …*(she shakes her head hard, the brightness slamming back down)* …*no.* …*no*, it's the *same* as *always.* *number* *ten.* he's *coming* *back* and he'll be *cross* we *cried.* …he *has* *to.* …he *always* …*(barely, the crack showing through)* …he *always* *has.**

nodes.speaker: Tamsin
nodes.text: *(You take her fluttering hands and you hold them still, and you do not rush, and you give her the hard truth wrapped in so much patience and so much warmth that it cannot be mistaken for cruelty — and you watch the glassy brightness meet the words and, for once, not deflect them, because you have made it safe enough to land. You watch her understand. It is the worst thing you have ever watched, and the kindest thing you have ever done.)* *(She goes very still. And then, slowly, like a building deciding to come down, the brightness *falls* — and what is underneath is a grief so total that she folds around it, sinking down out of the manic height she's been holding, all the way down to the cold ground, and you go down with her, still holding her hands.)* …it doesn't — *(barely, wrecked)* …it doesn't *send* him to the *Fugue.* …it *unwrites* him. …there's no — *(a sound tears out of her)* …there's no *door.* …i've been *waiting* at a *door* …and there's *no* *one* … …*(she grips your hands like the only fixed thing left)* …*oh*, cold one. …*oh*, it's — you were *right*, it's *worse*, it's so much *worse* than the *waiting* … …*but* — *(she gasps, through it)* …but you *stayed.* …you took me *down* …and you *didn't* *let* *go* of my *hands.* …*(she weeps, and it is bottomless, and it is honest, and it is the first true thing she's done since he fell)* …*Sela.* …is *Sela* — i *abandoned* her, didn't i, up on my — i *left* her *on* the *ground* *alone* while i — *(she looks toward Sela, stricken)* …i have to — we have to — *(she steadies, barely, a new and terrible clarity in her)* …*two* *of* *us* …*on* the *ground* …is *better* …than *one* *on* the *ground* …and *one* on the *ceiling.* …*help* me *up*, cold one. …not *up* off the — i don't mean *up*, i mean — …*help* me *over* …*to* *her.* …we'll *grieve* him *together.* …the *honest* *way.* …the way you only grieve …*(barely)* …what you've *finally* …*let* *yourself* …*lose.* …*thank* you. …it *broke* me. …*exactly* like you *said.* …and the *breaking* …*(a wet, ruined breath)* …the *breaking* *let* …the *light* *in.**

nodes.speaker: Tamsin
nodes.text: *(You hold her hands and you tell her the truth, gently, and it lands — not all at once, not cleanly, but it lands, and you watch the brittle brightness crack down the middle and the grief start, finally, to seep through.)* …*unmaking.* *(barely)* …*Coll's* word. …you're *saying* it *too.* …*(she doesn't pull away, but her hands shake in yours)* …you're *saying* …there's *no* *Fugue* for him. …no *sorting.* no — *(she presses her lips together, hard)* …no *coming* *back.* *(a long, awful silence, and then a single tear, and then she lets you see it)* …i *think* i *knew*, cold one. …*somewhere.* …i think i've *known* since the — since i *saw* the *grey* go *into* him and saw his *face* go — …*(she shudders)* …i just — i *couldn't* be the one to — to *say* it *first.* because *saying* it *makes* it — *(she swallows)* …*all* *right.* …*all* *right.* …i'll — i'll *try* to *let* it *be* *true.* …i can't — not *all* at *once*, cold one, i can't *do* it *all* at *once* … …but i'll *stop* *waiting* at the — *(her voice breaks)* …at the *door.* …i'll *try* to *stop.* …*stay* a *moment?* …*while* i — *(barely)* …*while* i *start* …to *put* my *feet* …on the *ground?**

nodes.speaker: Tamsin
nodes.text: *(You tell her the truth — and it is true, and you mean it kindly — but she is not ready, the thread is too taut, and the words hit it and it does not break so much as *recoil*, the brightness slamming back down harder than before, a soul protecting the one belief it cannot survive losing yet.)* …*no.* *(she pulls her hands back)* …*no*, i'll — i *said* i'll not *hear* it, and i *meant* *you* *too.* *(the smile is back, fixed, terrible)* …*'unmaking.'* you and *Coll*, with your — …you *want* me to *give* *up* on him. to *stop* *waiting.* to *do* the *one* thing he'd *never* *forgive*, which is *doubt* the *Last* *Word.* *(she's trembling)* …*nine* *times*, cold one. *nine.* and *every* time someone *gave* *up* …he came *back* and was *cross.* …i'll not — i'll *not* be the one who *gave* *up.* …i'll be the one who — who *kept* the *faith* …who was *waiting* …*whistling* …when he — *(her voice cracks, but she rebuilds the smile)* …*go.* …go and be *grim* with *Coll.* …*i'll* be *here.* …*believing.* …*someone* …*has* to.* …*(barely, beneath it)* …*please* …*someone* *has* to.*

nodes.speaker: Tamsin
nodes.text: *(You mean to be gentle — but it comes out too fast, too blunt, the truth without enough of the patience it needed, and the word 'unmaking' lands like a slap before she's braced for it, and you watch, in horror, as you do the precise thing Coll warned against: you don't take her down off the ceiling, you knock her off it.)* …*he's* — *(she flinches as though struck)* …you just — you *said* it like — *like* it was *nothing*, like *'your* friend is *erased*, *Tamsin*, there's *no* *door*' — *(she's gone white, the brightness shattered into something jagged and panicked)* …*why* would you — *(she backs away)* …i *asked* you to be *kind*, i *told* you the *thread* was — and you just *cut* it, you *cut* it *clean*, and now i'm — *(she's gasping, the terror underneath fully exposed, raw)* …now there's *nothing*, cold one, there's — if he can be *erased*, if even *he* — then *nothing's* *safe*, *nothing*, the *worst* *always* — *(she wraps her arms around herself, shaking violently)* …*go* *away.* …*please*, just — i can't — i can't have you *near* while i — *(barely, drowning)* …you were *right*, that's the — that's the *worst* of it, you were *right* …but you said it *wrong* …and *now* i have to *fall* …with *no* one *holding* — …*go.* …*find* *Coll.* …*tell* him to *come.* …*he'd* not have — *(a sob)* …*he'd* not have *said* it …*like* *that.**

nodes.speaker: Tamsin
nodes.text: *(She nods quickly, gratefully, clutching the thread.)* …*thank* you. …for not — *(a brittle smile)* …for not *pulling* it. *(she resumes her restless tidying, then pauses, and something honest slips through the brightness, just for a breath)* …i *know*, cold one. …*somewhere*, i — *(she touches the standing blade, lightly, the way you'd touch a sleeping thing you're afraid to wake)* …*Coll* keeps *counting.* and *Sela's* on the *ground.* and i'm up *here* *all* *alone* *believing* …and it's *getting* …*harder* …to *hold.* *(she pulls her hand back from the blade)* …when you *come* *back*, cold one … …*(barely)* …*maybe* …*maybe* *then* …i'll be *ready* …to *hear* …the *thing* …you *didn't* *say.* …not *yet.* …but — …*(a whisper)* …*soon*, i think. …the *thread's* …so *thin* *now.* …*come* *back* …and *be* *the* *one* …who's *there* …when it *finally* …*breaks.**
